name: component-contribution

metadata.name: component-contribution
metadata.summary: A method for estimating the standard reaction Gibbs energy of biochemical reactions

documentation:
# Component Contribution

[![PyPI version](https://badge.fury.io/py/component-contribution.svg)](https://badge.fury.io/py/component-contribution)
[![Anaconda-Server Badge](https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/component-contribution/badges/version.svg)](https://anaconda.org/conda-forge/component-contribution)
[![Python version](https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/component-contribution.svg)](https://www.python.org/downloads)
[![MIT license](https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/component-contribution.svg)](https://mit-license.org/)

[![pipeline status](https://gitlab.com/elad.noor/component-contribution/badges/develop/pipeline.svg)](https://gitlab.com/elad.noor/component-contribution/commits/develop)
[![codecov](https://codecov.io/gl/equilibrator/component-contribution/branch/develop/graph/badge.svg?token=OxxaCqgaLs)](https://codecov.io/gl/equilibrator/component-contribution)
[![Join our Google group](https://img.shields.io/badge/google_group-equilibrator_users-blue)](https://groups.google.com/g/equilibrator-users)
[![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/equilibrator/badge/?version=latest)](https://equilibrator.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest)

A method for estimating the standard reaction Gibbs energy of biochemical reactions. 

## Cite us

For more information on the method behind component-contribution, please view our open
access paper:

Noor E, Haraldsdóttir HS, Milo R, Fleming RMT (2013)
[Consistent Estimation of Gibbs Energy Using Component Contributions](http://journals.plos.org/ploscompbiol/article?id=10.1371/journal.pcbi.1003098),
PLoS Comput Biol 9:e1003098, DOI: 10.1371/journal.pcbi.1003098

Please, cite this paper if you publish work that uses `component-contribution`.

## Installation

* `pip install component-contribution`

## Dependencies

* Python 3.9+
* PyPI dependencies for prediction:
  - equilibrator-cache
  - numpy
  - scipy
  - pandas
  - pint
  - path
  - periodictable
  - uncertainties
* PyPI dependencies for training a new model:
  - openbabel
  - equilibrator-assets

## Data sources

* [Training data for the component contribution method](https://zenodo.org/record/3978440)
* [Chemical group definitions for the component-contribution method](https://zenodo.org/record/4010930)

modules.name: component_contribution.linalg
documentation: A file with some Linear Algebra tools.

classes.name: component_contribution.linalg.LINALG

methods.name: component_contribution.linalg.LINALG.invert_project
documentation:
Use SVD to invert a matrix, while computing the subspace projections.

Parameters
----------
A : np.ndarray
    A 2D NumPy array
tol : float, optional
    A threshold on the eigenvalues (below which are considered 0)
    (Default value = 1e-10)

Returns
-------
tuple
    a 4-tuple of (inv(A), rank, P_R, P_N) where the P_R is the
    projection onto the range of A, and P_N is the projection onto the
    nullspace of A.T

methods.name: component_contribution.linalg.LINALG.row_uniq
documentation:
Matrix Row Unique.

A procedure usually performed before linear regression (i.e. solving
Ax = y). If the matrix A contains repeating rows, it is advisable to
combine all of them to one row, and the observed value corresponding
to that row will be the average of the original observations.

Parameters
----------
A : np.ndarray
    A 2D NumPy array

Returns
-------
tuple
    a 2-tuple (A_unique, P_row) where A_unique has the same
    number of columns as A, but with unique rows. P_row is a matrix that
    can be used to map the original rows to the ones in A_unique (all
    values in P_row are 0 or 1).

methods.name: component_contribution.linalg.LINALG.col_uniq
documentation:
Matrix Column Unique.

A procedure usually performed before linear regression (i.e. solving
Ax = y). If the matrix A contains repeating columns, it is advisable to
combine all of them to one column, and the observed value corresponding
to that column will be the average of the original observations.

Parameters
----------
A : np.ndarray
    A 2D NumPy array

Returns
-------
tuple
    a 2-tuple (A_unique, P_col) where A_unique has the same
    number of rows as A, but with unique columns. P_col is a matrix that
    can be used to map the original columns to the ones in A_unique (all
    values in P_col are 0 or 1).

methods.name: component_contribution.linalg.LINALG.qr_rank_deficient
documentation:
Rank-revealing row elimination of A.

Uses the QR decomposition to find the solution to R.T @ R = A.T @ A,
where R is fully ranked.

Parameters
----------
A : np.array
    A n-by-m matrix with rank r < min(n, m)
tol : float, optional
    The tolerance below which an eigenvalue is considered 0.
    The default is 1e-10.

Returns
-------
R : np.array
    A matrix of size r-by-m, that satisfies R.T @ R = A.T @ A
Some general linear algebra that's missing from scipy.

modules.name: component_contribution.trainer
documentation: Component Contribution Training script.

classes.name: component_contribution.trainer.Trainer

methods.name: component_contribution.trainer.Trainer.train
documentation:
Create a GibbsEnergyPredictor object.

Parameters
----------
training_data : TrainingData
    a TrainingData object containing all training data

Returns
-------
CCModelParameters
    The parameters of the Component Contribution model.

methods.name: component_contribution.trainer.Trainer.group_incidence_matrix
documentation:
Initialize G matrix.

Decompose each of the compounds that has an InChI into groups,
and save the decomposition as a row in the G matrix.
Compounds that cannot be decomposed are added as new "groups",
i.e. as extra columns to the matrix.

training_data : TrainingData
    a TrainingData object containing all training data

Returns
-------
pd.DataFrame
    The group incidence matrix as a pd.DataFrame.

methods.name: component_contribution.trainer.Trainer.train_from_matrices
documentation:
Estimate standard Gibbs energies of formation.

Parameters
----------
train_S : pd.DataFrame
    training data stoichiometric matrix
train_G : pd.DataFrame
    group incidence matrix
train_b : pd.Series
    training data reverse transformed dG'0 values
train_w : pd.Series
    weights
number_of_groups : int
    the number of "real" groups

Returns
-------
CCModelParameters
    Collection of Component Contribution parameters trained using
    the input data.
A class for training the component contribution model.

modules.name: component_contribution.preprocessor
documentation: calculations needed for component-contribution predictions.

classes.name: component_contribution.preprocessor.Preprocessor

methods.name: component_contribution.preprocessor.Preprocessor.__init__
documentation:
Create a GibbsEnergyPredictor object.

Parameters
----------
parameters : CCModelParameters
    all the parameters needed for running Component Contribution
    predictions.
rmse_inf : float
    The MSE of the subspace that's not covered by Component Contribution
    which is set as an arbitrary high value. By default we set it to
    10^10 (kJ^2 / mol^2)

methods.name: component_contribution.preprocessor.Preprocessor.get_compound_vector
documentation:
Get a vector representation of the compound.

Parameters
----------
compound : Compound
    a Compound object


Returns
-------
x : np.ndarray (optional)
    a 1-D array representing of the compound in the component-
    contribution space. The first Nc dimensions are for the reactant-
    contribution space, and can be seen as compound indicators. The
    following Ng dimensions are the group contributions. If the
    compound is not in the training set (self._compound_ids)
    and also doesn't have a group vector, the return value is None.

methods.name: component_contribution.preprocessor.Preprocessor.get_compound_prediction
documentation:
Get the prediction for a compound's formation energy.

Parameters
----------
compound : Compound
    the input Reaction object


Returns
-------
mu : float
    the mean of the standard formation Gibbs energy estimate
sigma_fin : array
    a vector representing the square root of the covariance matrix
    (uncertainty)
sigma_inf : array
    a vector representing the infinite-uncertainty eigenvalues of the
    covariance matrix

methods.name: component_contribution.preprocessor.Preprocessor.get_reaction_prediction
documentation:
Get the prediction for a reaction's energy.

Parameters
----------
reaction : Reaction
    the input Reaction object


Returns
-------
mu : float
    the mean of the standard reaction Gibbs energy estimate
sigma_fin : array
    a vector representing the square root of the covariance matrix
    (uncertainty)
sigma_inf : array
    a vector representing the infinite-uncertainty eigenvalues of the
    covariance matrix
residual : dict
    the residual reaction in sparse notation (unknown and
    undecomposable reactants)

methods.name: component_contribution.preprocessor.Preprocessor._residuals_to_matrix
documentation:
Construct the residual stoichiometric matrix U.

Parameters
----------
residuals : List[dict]
    a list of dictionaries with each residual reaction in sparse
    notation (unknown and undecomposable reactants)

Returns
-------
sigma_res : np.array
    a matrix with the square root of the covariance corresponding
    to the 'unknown' compounds (i.e. where the uncertainty is infinite)

methods.name: component_contribution.preprocessor.Preprocessor.get_reaction_prediction_multi
documentation:
Get the (mu, sigma) predictions of a reaction's energy.

Parameters
----------
reactions: List[Reaction] :
    a list of Reaction objects


Returns
-------
mus : np.array
    the mean of the standard Gibbs energy estimates
sigmas_fin : np.array
    a matrix whose columns are the square root vectors (one for each
    reaction) of the uncertainty
sigmas_inf : np.array
    a matrix whose columns represent the infinite-uncertainty
    eigenvalues of the covariance
sigma_res : np.array
    another matrix with the square root of the covariance corresponding
    to the 'unknown' compounds (i.e. where the uncertainty is infinite)

methods.name: component_contribution.preprocessor.Preprocessor.decompose_reaction
documentation:
Decompose a reaction.

Parameters
----------
reaction : Reaction
    the input Reaction object


Returns
-------
x : ndarray
    the reaction vector (describing both RC and GC changes)
residual : dict
    the residual reaction in sparse notation

methods.name: component_contribution.preprocessor.Preprocessor.dg_analysis
documentation:
Analyse the weight of each observation to the $\Delta G$.

# Parameters

reaction

: the input Reaction object

# Returns

list

: a list of reactions that contributed to the value of the
  $\Delta G$ estimation, with their weights and extra
  information

methods.name: component_contribution.preprocessor.Preprocessor.is_using_group_contribution
documentation:
Check if group contributions is needed for the $\Delta G$.

# Parameters

reaction

: the input Reaction object

# Returns

bool

: True if the reaction require group contributions.

methods.name: component_contribution.preprocessor.Preprocessor.get_reaction_prediction_orthogonal_dof
documentation:
Get the sigma_inf of a list reactions.

Parameters
----------
reactions: List[Reaction] :
    a list of Reaction objects


Returns
-------
sigmas : np.array
    matrix whose columns (representing reactions) contain the degrees
    of freedom in the CC orthogonal space.
A Component Contribution preprocessing class.

methods.name: component_contribution.training_data.TrainingData._balance_reaction
documentation:
Try balancing the reaction with H2O and H+.

```
param rxn

A Reaction object

return

A balanced Reaction, or None
```

methods.name: component_contribution.training_data.TrainingData.balance_reactions
documentation:
Balance the reactions.

Use the chemical formulas from the InChIs to verify that each and
every reaction is balanced. If it can be easily fixed with protons
and/or water, fix it. Otherwise, drop this reaction from the training
data.

methods.name: component_contribution.training_data.TrainingData.create_stoichiometric_matrix_from_reactions
documentation:
Create the stoichiometric matrix for training.

Convert the list of reactions in sparse notation into a full
stoichiometric matrix, where the rows (compounds) are in the same
order as in 'compounds' and the columns match the reaction indices.

methods.name: component_contribution.training_data.TrainingData._reverse_transform
documentation:
Reverse Legendre transform.

Parameters

override_ionic_strength : bool, optional

    files with this value.
override_temperature : bool, optional
    If provided, overrides all temperature values given in the data
    files with this value.
override_p_mg : bool, optional
    If provided, overrides all p_mg values given in the data
    files with this value.
A base class for handling Training Data.

methods.name: component_contribution.training_data.FullTrainingDataFactory.read_tecrdb
documentation:
Load a data frame with information from the TECRdb (NIST).

The component-contribution package distributes data tables with
information on the 'thermodynamics of enzyme-catalyzed
reactions'[1, 2]\_ that are used as training data.

```
return

a Pandas DataFrame of the TECRDB reaction
```

# References

[^footnote-1]: Goldberg, Robert N., Yadu B. Tewari, and Talapady N. Bhat.
    “Thermodynamics of Enzyme-Catalyzed Reactions—a Database for
    Quantitative Biochemistry.” Bioinformatics 20, no. 16
    (November 1, 2004): 2874–77.
    <https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/bth314>.

[^footnote-2]: <http://xpdb.nist.gov/enzyme_thermodynamics/>


of compounds that do not decompose into groups.

All the reduction potentials we have from [7-14].

```

a Pandas DataFrame of the redox reactions.
```
Define a factory for the full training data.

modules.name: component_contribution.predict
documentation: wrapper for component-contribution predictions.

classes.name: component_contribution.predict.GibbsEnergyPredictor

methods.name: component_contribution.predict.GibbsEnergyPredictor.__init__
documentation:
Create a GibbsEnergyPredictor object.

parameters : CCModelParameters
    Optional CC parameters. If not provided, the parameters are
    automatically downloaded from Zenodo.
rmse_inf : Qunatity
    another parameter determining the uncertainty we associate
    with reactions that are not covered at all (not by RC nor GC)
    (Default value = 1e5 kJ/mol)

methods.name: component_contribution.predict.GibbsEnergyPredictor.get_compound_prediction
documentation: Get the (mu, sigma) predictions of a compound's formation energy.

methods.name: component_contribution.predict.GibbsEnergyPredictor.get_reaction_prediction
documentation:
Get the (mu, sigma) predictions of a reaction's energy.

See the Preprocessor class for details.

methods.name: component_contribution.predict.GibbsEnergyPredictor.combine_uncertainties
documentation:
Calculate the transformed reaction energies of a list of reactions.

Parameters
----------
sigmas_fin : np.array
    a matrix whose columns are the square root vectors (one for each
    reaction) of the uncertainty
sigmas_inf : np.array
    a matrix whose columns represent the infinite-uncertainty
    eigenvalues of the covariance
sigma_res : np.array
    another matrix with the square root of the covariance corresponding
    to the 'unknown' compounds (i.e. where the uncertainty is infinite)
uncertainty_representation : str
    which representation to use for the uncertainties.
    `cov` would return a full covariance matrix.
    `precision` would return the precision matrix
    (i.e. the inverse of the covariance matrix).
    `sqrt` would return a sqaure root of the covariance,
    based on the uncertainty vectors.
    `fullrank` would return a full-rank square root of the covariance
    which is a compressed form of the `sqrt` result.
    (Default value: `cov`)

Returns
-------
dg_uncertainty : Quantity
    the uncertainty matrix (in either `cov`, `precision`, `sqrt or
    `fullrank` format)

methods.name: component_contribution.predict.GibbsEnergyPredictor.standard_dgf
documentation:
Calculate the chemical formation energy of the major MS at pH 7.

Parameters
----------
compound: Compound
    a compound object


Returns
-------
Measurement
    a tuple of two arrays. the first is a 1D NumPy array
    containing the CC estimates for the reactions' untransformed dG0
    (i.e. using the major MS at pH 7 for each of the reactants).
    the second is a 2D numpy array containing the covariance matrix
    of the standard errors of the estimates. one can use the
    eigenvectors of the matrix to define a confidence high-dimensional
    space, or use U as the covariance of a Gaussian used for sampling
    (where dG0_cc is the mean of that Gaussian).

methods.name: component_contribution.predict.GibbsEnergyPredictor.standard_dg
documentation:
Calculate the chemical reaction energy.

Using the major microspecies of each of the reactants.

# Parameters

reaction: Reaction :

: the input Reaction object

# Returns

Measurement
standard_dg : Measurement

> the $\Delta G$ in kJ/mol and standard error. to
> calculate the 95% confidence interval, multiply the error by 1.96

methods.name: component_contribution.predict.GibbsEnergyPredictor.standard_dg_multi
documentation:
Calculate the chemical reaction energies for a list of reactions.

Using the major microspecies of each of the reactants.

# Parameters

reactions

: a list of Reaction objects

uncertainty_representation

: which representation to use for the uncertainties.
  `cov` would return a full covariance matrix.
  `precision` would return the precision matrix
  (i.e. the inverse of the covariance matrix).
  `sqrt` would return a sqaure root of the covariance,
  based on the uncertainty vectors.
  `fullrank` would return a full-rank square root of the covariance
  which is a compressed form of the `sqrt` result.
  (Default value: `cov`)

# Returns

standard_dg

: the array of Component Contribution estimates for the reactions'
  untransformed $\Delta G$ in kJ/mol,
  (i.e. using the major MS at pH 7 for each of the reactants).

dg_uncertainty

: the uncertainty matrix (in either `cov`, `precision`, `` sqrt or
  `fullrank `` format)

methods.name: component_contribution.predict.GibbsEnergyPredictor.standard_dgf_prime
documentation:
Calculate the biocheimcal formation energy of the compound.

Parameters
----------
compound : Compound
    a compound object
p_h : Quantity
    Set the -log10 of the proton concentration [H+].
ionic_strength : Quantity
    Set the ionic strength.
temperature : Quantity
    Set the temperature.
p_mg : Quantity
    Set the -log10 of the magnesium ion concentration [Mg++].
    (Default value = default_pMg)


Returns
-------
type
    a tuple of two arrays. the first is a 1D NumPy array
    containing the CC estimates for the reactions' untransformed dG0
    (i.e. using the major MS at pH 7 for each of the reactants).
    the second is a 2D numpy array containing the covariance matrix
    of the standard errors of the estimates. one can use the
    eigenvectors of the matrix to define a confidence high-dimensional
    space, or use U as the covariance of a Gaussian used for sampling
    (where dG0_cc is the mean of that Gaussian).

methods.name: component_contribution.predict.GibbsEnergyPredictor.standard_dg_prime
documentation:
Calculate the transformed reaction energies of a reaction.

# Parameters

reaction

: the input Reaction object

p_h

: Set the -log10 of the proton concentration [H+].

ionic_strength

: Set the ionic strength.

temperature

: Set the temperature.

p_mg

: Set the -log10 of the magnesium ion concentration [Mg++].
  (Default value = default_pMg)

# Returns

standard_dg

: the $\Delta G'$ in kJ/mol and standard error. to
  calculate the 95% confidence interval, multiply the error by 1.96

methods.name: component_contribution.predict.GibbsEnergyPredictor.standard_dg_prime_multi
documentation:
Calculate the transformed reaction energies of a list of reactions.

# Parameters

reactions

: a list of Reaction objects

p_h

: Set the -log10 of the proton concentration [H+].

ionic_strength

: Set the ionic strength.

temperature

: Set the temperature.

p_mg

: Set the -log10 of the magnesium ion concentration [Mg++].
  (Default value = default_pMg)

# Returns

standard_dg_prime

: the array of Component Contribution estimates for the reactions'
  $\Delta G'$ in kJ/mol

cov_dg

: the covariance matrix of the standard errors of the estimates.
  one can use the eigenvectors of the matrix to define a confidence
  high-dimensional space, or use U as the covariance of a Gaussian
  used for sampling (where cov_dg is the mean of that Gaussian).

methods.name: component_contribution.predict.GibbsEnergyPredictor.multicompartmental_standard_dg_prime
documentation:
Calculate the transformed energies of multi-compartmental reactions.

Based on the equations from
Harandsdottir et al. 2012 (<https://doi.org/10.1016/j.bpj.2012.02.032>)

# Parameters

reaction_1

: half-reaction associated to compartment 1

reaction_2

: half-reaction associated to compartment 2

transported_protons

: the total number of protons
  transported through the membrane

transported_charge

: the total charge
  transported through the membrane

p_h_1

: the pH in compartment 1

p_h_2

: the pH in compartment 2

ionic_strength_1

: the ionic strength in compartment 1

ionic_strength_2

: the ionic strength in compartment 2

e_potential_difference

: electrostatic potential across the membrane

temperature

: temperature in Kalvin

p_mg_1

: the pMg in compartment 1 (Default value = 10)

p_mg_2

: the pMg in compartment 2 (Default value = 10)

# Returns

standard_dg

: the $\Delta G'$ in kJ/mol and standard error. to
  calculate the 95% confidence interval, multiply the error by 1.96

methods.name: component_contribution.predict.GibbsEnergyPredictor.dg_analysis
documentation:
Analyse the weight of each observation to the $\Delta G$.

# Parameters

reaction

: the input Reaction object

# Returns

list

: a list of reactions that contributed to the value of the
  $\Delta G$ estimation, with their weights and extra
  information

methods.name: component_contribution.predict.GibbsEnergyPredictor.is_using_group_contribution
documentation:
Check if group contributions is needed for the $\Delta G$.

# Parameters

reaction

: the input Reaction object

# Returns

bool

: True if the reaction require group contributions.

methods.name: component_contribution.predict.GibbsEnergyPredictor.dgf_prime_sensitivity_to_p_h
documentation:
Calculate the derivative of the reaction ΔG' w.r.t. pH.

By derivating the Legendre transform as a function of pH, we get
the slope which is a linear approximation of the pH response.

# Parameters

compound

: a compound object

p_h

: Set the -log10 of the proton concentration [H+].

ionic_strength

: Set the ionic strength.

temperature

: Set the temperature.

p_mg

: Set the -log10 of the magnesium ion concentration [Mg++].
  (Default value = default_pMg)

# Returns

Quantity

: The derivative of $\Delta G_r$ with respect to pH.

methods.name: component_contribution.predict.GibbsEnergyPredictor.dg_prime_sensitivity_to_p_h
documentation:
Calculate the derivative of the reaction ΔG' w.r.t. pH.

By derivating the Legendre transform as a function of pH, we get
the slope which is a linear approximation of the pH response.

# Parameters

reaction

: the input Reaction object

p_h

: Set the -log10 of the proton concentration [H+].

ionic_strength

: Set the ionic strength.

temperature

: Set the temperature.

p_mg

: Set the -log10 of the magnesium ion concentration [Mg++].
  (Default value = default_pMg)

# Returns

Quantity

: The derivative of $\Delta G_r$ with respect to pH.
A class that can be used to predict dGs of reactions using CC.
